channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A Blizzard With Record Snowfall for Parts of the Northeast
item.description: At the storm’s peak, well over half a million homes and businesses in the Northeast were without power.

item.title: Where patients live matters for access to gene therapy
item.description: Gene therapies have the potential to cure some diseases, but they are extraordinarily expensive. Location can also be a big hurdle for patients seekin...

item.title: Is the YIMBY movement doomed?
item.description: For decades, rising home prices have been an engine for middle-class wealth. Now a growing movement wants to slow — or even reverse — that trend. Are ...

item.title: State of the Union May Be a Trial for ‘Barely Invited’ Justices
item.description: If tradition holds, members of the Supreme Court will attend Trump’s annual speech, just days after ruling against the legality of his tariffs.

item.title: DOJ hides Trump-related Epstein files. And, what to know for the State of the Union
item.description: An NPR investigation found that the DOJ withheld some Epstein files related to abuse allegations against Trump. And, what you need to know ahead of th...

item.title: Supreme Court Sides With Couple in Case Involving Baby Food Sold at Whole Foods
item.description: The court agreed to revive a lawsuit by a Texas couple who claimed that tainted baby food purchased at Whole Foods had sickened their young son.

item.title: Democrats tap Spanberger and Padilla to respond to State of the Union
item.description: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger will deliver Democrats' response on Tuesday following President Trump's State of the Union address.

item.title: A U.S. veteran adopted an orphan from Iran. Decades later, ICE is trying to deport her
item.description: The woman has no criminal record and is unsure what prompted the threat of removal. She fears being deported to Iran given her father's military servi...

item.title: Robert Carradine, Actor Who Played the Father in ‘Lizzie McGuire,’ Dies at 71
item.description: A member of a renowned acting dynasty, he also earned fame for his role in “Revenge of the Nerds.” His family said he struggled with bipolar disorder.

item.title: Meta Announces Major Chips-for-Stock Deal With AMD
item.description: The multibillion-dollar deal is AMD’s latest move to catch up to Nvidia in the lucrative world of selling artificial intelligence chips.

item.title: Russia Moves Closer to Banning Telegram App, Saying It Foments Terrorism
item.description: News of an investigation into Pavel Durov, the app’s billionaire founder, deepened a crackdown on what remains of the free Russian internet.

item.title: Mandelson’s Arrest Adds to Scandal That Has Rattled Starmer and Labour Party
item.description: The arrest of the British former envoy to Washington, long a key figure in the Labour Party, deepened a scandal that has led to calls for Prime Minist...

item.title: Mexico Is Caught Between Trump and the Cartels
item.description: President Trump has demanded President Claudia Sheinbaum confront the cartels. The killing of “El Mencho” suggests it might be working — but could com...

item.title: Airlines Add Flights After Storm and Snow in Northeast Cause Cancellations
item.description: The major U.S. airlines said they would add more flights in airports that serve cities like Boston, New York and Philadelphia.

item.title: After Six Decades of the War on Drugs, What Works?
item.description: The U.S. and its allies have spilled blood and treasure to kill drug lords and defeat cartels, but the drugs keep coming and the new groups are more v...
